front: Poem: the speaker advances from fire to cities and highways, then concludes he should go back 'to that rock that was home, and start scratching all over again'
back: "The Soliloquy of the Individual" / Poems and Antipoems (Nicanor Parra)

front: Author: this Chilean anti-poet's landmark 1954 collection was translated by Lawrence Ferlinghetti and Allen Ginsberg
Poem: a series of visual poem postcards including 'Bargain crucifix, for sale' and 'U.S.A., where liberty is a statue'; includes 'Lyric Poet Pithecantropos Erectus' satirizing Neruda
Author: this poet was famous for saying 'I take back everything I've said' after readings; one antipoem ends by urging 'generous reader, burn this book'
Author: Roberto Bolano wrote 'Eight Seconds with [this poet],' saying his poetry 'will endure'
Author: jokingly listed Chile's 'four great poets' as 'three: Alonso de Ercilla and Ruben Dario' (Dario is Nicaraguan)
Author: Thomas Merton called this Chilean anti-poet the 'poet of the sneeze'
Author: also a theoretical physicist/mathematician; gave an academic speech in front of Neruda noting the contradiction with being an 'anti-poet'
Poem: declares 'The poets have come down from Olympus'
Poem: opens 'A new era begins now, with the burning of the dirigible R101 as it crashes to earth'
